title: What's the best way to update sales targets for the new year in Monday CRM?
excerpt: I’m looking to update my sales targets on the Sales Operations section of Monday CRM for the new year 2026. The common advice seems to be to duplicate the Sales Targets board and add new targets, thereby retaining the hi…

posts.author: TysonM (Tyson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m looking to update my sales targets on the Sales Operations section of Monday CRM for the new year 2026. The common advice seems to be to duplicate the Sales Targets board and add new targets, thereby retaining the history from last year, however, the new board has connected columns to my Opportunities board to bring in Actual values from won deals each month. I therefore have Actuals data for December in my Sales Targets actuals, yet these values are for December 2025.
I can’t think of a way of doing this unless I duplicate my Opportunities board to make a 2026 version, but then I will have to modify all automations to work with the new board when Leads are qualified.
Surely there is a better way than having to duplicate every board for 2026? How is everyone else handling this new year, new targets and tracking scenario?

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @TysonM Totally hear you. Duplicating the Opportunities board usually creates more work than it’s worth, and most teams avoid that.

The cleaner approach is to keep one Opportunities board as your source of truth and separate targets by year, not by board. Add 2026 items or a Year column on your Sales Targets board, then make sure Actuals only count deals won within the correct year using a close date or won date. This prevents December 2025 deals from rolling into 2026 targets.

If you’re using dashboards, you can also filter widgets by close date year, which keeps reporting clean without changing your automations.

Many teams do duplicate the Sales Targets board for a new year, but reconnect it to the same Opportunities board and rely on date based filtering.

If you’d like hands-on help or want us to walk through this live, you can book a 1:1 paid 60-minute strategy session with our team here:
Calendly

posts.author: nichcraft (Wahed)
posts.cookedHtml: You can try to set up automations like “When status = Won AND Close Date in Jan 2026 → connect to Jan 2026 target item.” One per month, and each target row only pulls its own period’s actuals

posts.author: alex_m_89 (Alex Miller)
posts.cookedHtml:
One approach that’s worked well for us: add a “Year” dropdown column to your Sales Targets board (2025, 2026, etc.) and a matching “Close Year” formula column on Opportunities that extracts the year from Close Date.

Then use a single connected column between Targets and Opportunities, but filter your dashboard widgets by Close Year = 2026. That way your Actuals automatically only pull current-year deals without touching automations.

For the targets themselves, you can duplicate just the target rows (not the board), update the Year to 2026, and set new values. The 2025 rows stay as historical reference on the same board.

This avoids the board-duplication spiral entirely and keeps all your automations intact since they still point to the same Opportunities board.

posts.author: TysonM (Tyson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Alex, this is exactly the solution I used. This retains the history and I have the Sales person/year as rows on the Sales Target board. E.g. Paul Smith 2025, Paul Smith 2026.

It’s still not turn-key and a little involved and I really wish Monday.com had a much easier solution that enables the CRM to go beyond year 1 natively out of the box.

posts.author: arjumehta (Arju)
posts.cookedHtml: Use Goals/Numbers columns in Monday CRM to set new annual targets, duplicate last year’s board, adjust quotas, update dashboards, automate progress tracking, and notify sales reps of revised objectives.

posts.author: nitesh (Nitesh Singh)
posts.cookedHtml: Glad Alex’s approach worked for you Tyson. The year + salesperson row pattern is the most practical workaround right now. The underlying issue is that monday.com’s CRM doesn’t natively support multi-year target periods — it was clearly built for a single continuous board rather than annual cycles. Worth submitting this as a feature request if you haven’t already, specifically asking for a “target period” date range on the Sales Targets board so Actuals filter automatically by period without needing a year column workaround. More votes on that request increases the chance it gets prioritised.
